description: Validate wind or force-field driven drift for light rigid bodies or buoyant objects using explicit wind vector labels and trajectory displacement evidence. The balloon case is only a smoke family.
physical_assumptions: Wind is a structured force-field control, not a hidden animation path.
Wind-driven objects must declare mass or buoyancy-sensitive physical properties.
Observed horizontal drift should align with the wind vector within expected displacement bounds.
Vertical motion should stay inside the declared altitude envelope unless another force is specified.
verifier_rules: Expected wind vector exists and has non-zero horizontal magnitude.
Wind subject trajectory has at least two frames.
Projected displacement along wind direction stays within expected range.
Drift opposite the wind vector is rejected.
Altitude remains inside the expected envelope when specified.
repair_suggestions: Move wind speed and direction into `expected_physics.wind_vector_m_s` or `physics_control.force_field.wind`.
Bind the wind subject as a physics-critical light body, not a visual-only sprite.
Check that runtime exports force-field labels and trajectory frames before tuning wind speed.
Use negative cases for missing wind labels, no drift, and wrong drift direction.
